title: gymnogramma capensis
intro: Gymnogramma capensis, also known as 'Horse's Teeth' or 'Tongue Fern', is a type of succulent plant that belongs to the family Asteraceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Gymnogramma capensis include treating various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as being used as an antiseptic to prevent infections. Gymnogramma capensis can be prepared as a tea or infusion by steeping the leaves in hot water, and it is also used in traditional herbal remedies to treat a range of health issues.
